updates.tweet_text:
We got our injection of volatility and now chop in #ES_F. Levels respecting perfectly. 7574-77 was support of a range with 7620 resistance. We sold to 7574 *exact* - longs triggered - and up we went. 7604, 7620 if ES wants more, then multi-day break.

Next dip is under 7574 https://t.co/hagTYBMj2p
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7574-77, 7604, 7620) are observations of price action or reaffirmations of morning-plan levels; 7574 dip reference is a hindsight/observation note, not a new actionable level.

updates.tweet_text:
As posted, we got our morning spike in volatility, now chop in #ES_F. Levels respecting perfectly. The range is 7574-77 to 7620. At 9:45AM, we held 7574 *exact* and rallied 23 points. Now back again, weaker this time.

7593, 7604, 7620 still above. 7565, 7558 if we can break down https://t.co/GAbcU6l4LS

updates.tweet_text: @NYJets2017 @tradivate I just track the technicals and broad cycles. Right now we are quite obviously early in this particular cycle and we just broke out of a multi-month flag in April. There will be 3-4 repetitions of that still like all bull markets.

updates.tweet_text:
Back to chop in #ES_F and this market is all about two levels: 7574-77 range support & 7620 range resistance. At 9:45AM we held 7574 exact and rallied 23 points. Now, were back again after trapping it. 7577 reclaim with force takes back to 7587, 7604+

7565, 7558 remain supports https://t.co/RmAbbGLGLB
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7574-77, 7620, 7587, 7604, 7565, 7558) are either morning-plan levels or observations of price action; no new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Nice level to level move. I posted 2hrs ago that 7577 must reclaim to take ES back to 7587 (hit), 7604. We remain rangebound 7577-74 to 7620 and 7577-74 is a massive range. Newsletter out soon talking next entries from here.
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7577, 7587, 7604, 7620) are morning-plan levels; tweet is a hindsight recap with no new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @AlbanoRick That's not my edge! I did that for many years and it cost me win rate. Its not as simple as "shorting when its going down". If you add a new trade type like that, those trades have an associated win rate, its typically far lower, and drags down my total win rate and R/R
updates.notes: Tweet is a reply about trading methodology/edge philosophy. No prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @AlbanoRick No problem. I could re-add breakdown shorts to my setups for example, but with Failed Breakdowns I am at a 95%+ win rate. Breakdown Shorts I'd be something like 40-60%. In addition, I'd have to miss the Failed Breakdown/squeeze after the short. So I am 1) Lowering my avg win rate
updates.notes: Reply explaining trading strategy philosophy (FBD vs breakdown shorts, win rates). No price levels named or actionable triggers mentioned.

updates.tweet_text:
We finally got a little vol overnight. All week the  #ES_F range was 7574 (which paid us 5x) to 7620. Overnight, it finally lost. Per newsletter, traps of 7527 were buyable & we just got it

Plan: 7558, 7565, 7575 next (bulls must recover). 7527 (watch traps)=support. Sell below https://t.co/kmOQvxMS4a
updates.notes: All prices cited (7527, 7558, 7563/7565≈7563, 7574/7575≈7574.5, 7620) are already in the morning plan; tweet is a reaffirmation/recap, not new levels.

updates.tweet_text: @DavidBohn11 I don't do that unless I'm in a trade. I have stops set on my existing trades but I don't really care what price does in the middle of the night If I'm not actively trading. I only look to take 5 or so points out a day and I can usually find that during my usual trading hours
updates.notes: Reply tweet explaining trading habits/schedule. No prices mentioned, no actionable levels of any kind.

updates.tweet_text: @PotsieWeber1949 @DavidBohn11 Its mostly the same every trade, I cut it in half though when volatility is extremely elevated  but we haven't had conditions to warrant that since March
updates.notes: Reply about position sizing/volatility management. No prices mentioned, no actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
Last evening we finally broke down the multi-day range which was 7574 to 7620. First stop down was 7527 per yesterday's 4pm newsletter. We held it exact all night, then trapped below at 7:40AM to trigger long.

No change from 8am post. 7558, 7565, 7575 next. Bulls must recover 75 https://t.co/Fp8HJOeBHt
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7527, 7558, 7565/7574, 7575/7574.5, 7576) are in the morning plan or are hindsight/reaffirmation references. No new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text:
Good follow through and there's 7558 1st target hit in #ES_F. The setup was the Failed Breakdown of 7527 which was a massive support shelf. We got it at 7:40Am. Targets today were 7558 (hit), 7565, 7575 (must must recover to bottom it out).

7527=support still, sell below https://t.co/JyyUJroV76
